text:
The 2024 Paris Olympic Games are well underway and Express Sport brings you live updates of the medal table.

Team GB won 64 medals at the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games; 22 gold, 20 silver and 22 bronze.

The most medals Team GB has won at a recent Olympic Games was in Rio de Janeiro 2016 when they finished second in the table with 27 golds, 23 silvers and 17 bronzes.

Stay tuned in below for all the latest medal news...

Adam Burgess takes silver for Team GB in the canoe slalom men's singles final with a time of 96.84 seconds.

Burgess was in a gold medal position until France's Nicolas Gestin produced a time of 91.36 seconds with the final run of the day.

Slovakia's Matej Benus gets bronze.

Just like that, Team GB have grabbed their second gold.

And it comes in the form of Tom Pidcock, who produced an epic comeback to win the mountain bike final in Paris, beating France's Victor Koretzky to Olympics glory.

Meanwhile, Laura Collett has grabbed bronze for Team GB in the individual eventing jumping final.

Team GB have claimed theirfirst gold medalin Paris in the eventing team final.

The trio of Tom McEwen, Laura Collett and Ros Canter starred, ending Team GB's wait for gold as they pipped France to the post, with Japan finishing third.

Tom Daley and Noah Williams have clinched silver for Team GB in the men's synchronised 10m diving.

The Team GB duo missed out on gold to Chinese duo Junjie Lian and Hao Yang.

South Korea have moved momentarily up to second in the standings after picking up their fourth gold medal of the Olympics.

Ban Hyojin won gold in the 10m Air Rifle event for South Korea.

Agonising scenes in the 100m breaststroke final, as Team GB's Adam Peaty puts on a masterful swim but is unable to secure the three-peat and a historic third consecutive Olympic gold medal.

He takes joint second and silver with American star Nic Fink.

Winning by fractions of miliseconds is Italian Nicolo Martinenghi, who takes gold.

The medal haul for Team GB continues, as Kimberley Woods takes bronze in the women's kayak singles final.

A nightmare for the defending champion. Germany's Ricarda Funk hits one of the gates and gets a time penalty.

That means Great Britain star Woods takes home the bronze medal.

The Parisian crowd goes wild as Pauline Ferrand-Prevot wins gold in the women's mountain bike.

It was a complete domination, finished two minutes and 57 seconds ahead of silver medalist Haley Batten, of the USA.

Sweden's Jenny Rissveds takes bronze.

South Korea add a gold and silver to their tally with a one-two in the women's 10m air pistol.

Oh Ye Jin scoops the gold and Kim Yeji adds the silver.

Manu Bhaker, of India, takes home the bronze.

It's a gold medal and a title defence for Ariarne Titmus in the 400m freestyle.

The 23-year-old led from the start and managed to hold off Summer MacIntosh of Canada, who takes silver.

USA star Katie Ledecky was well off the pace, but takes bronze.

A huge achievement for the hosts, who stun Fiji to take the gold medal on day one.

Belgium have announced themselves in Paris, picking up a gold medal in the cycling road event, along with a bronze. Won by Remco Evenepoel and Wout van Aert respectively.

Japan are now also off the mark, taking gold (Women's -48kg Natsumi Tsunoda) and bronze medals (Men's -60kg Ryuju Nagayama) in the Judo.

Italy's Fillipo Gianni collects bronze in the Men's Individual Time Trial in road cycling, placing them onto the medal board.

Mongolio are now also among the winners circle, taking a silver medal in the Women's -48kg Judo courtesy of Baasankhuu Bavuudorj.

Also in the Women's -48kg Judo, Sweden collect a bronze medal with Tara Babulfath.

Anna Henderson wins silver in the wmen's road race time trial! It's Team GB's second medal of the day and they're up to third in the table.

Grace Brown of Australia takes home the gold and American Chloe Dygert grabs bronze.

It's been pretty quiet on the medal table over the last couple of hours with many of the heats taking place.

The third medal event of the day takes place in the women’s individual time trial cycling at 1.30pm.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Scarlett Mew Jensen and Yasmin Harper snatched an unlikely bronze in women's diving - and it's the first female diving medal for Team GB in 64 years!

A huge error by the Australians in their final dive handed Team GB the medal place.

China secure the gold and USA silver.

China have won the first gold as Huang Yuting and Sheng Lihao beat South Korea 16-12 in the air rifle competition.

Kazakhstan secure third.

author: Mikael McKenzie, Danny Gallagher
